title: Request runtime permissions in context
description: Declare permissions in the manifest and request dangerous ones at point of use, handling denial and don't-ask-again.
body: Use when accessing protected data/features (location, camera, contacts, notifications). Do not access a dangerous-permission API assuming it is granted, and do not request everything up front. Actions: declare permissions in AndroidManifest, request dangerous ones at runtime via the permission launcher at the moment they are needed, show a rationale when appropriate, and degrade gracefully on denial (including permanent denial). Validation signals include the feature working after grant and not crashing on denial. Risk boundary: using a dangerous API without the granted runtime permission throws a SecurityException, and requesting unused permissions erodes trust and risks Play policy issues — request the minimum, in context, and handle every denial path.
